title: Multiply 3- and 4-digits by 1-digit with area models
description: Use an area model to decompose the larger factor and multiply.
topic_path: Multi-digit multiplication: place value and area models
Multiply 3- and 4-digits by 1-digit with area models

assessment_items.question_content:
The following area model shows $2\times459$ broken into parts.   
*The image is not drawn to scale.*

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each rectangle.**

$A=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$B=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

$C=$  [[☃ numeric-input 3]]

**Find the total area.**

$2\times459=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
The area of the whole rectangle is $2 \times 459$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Now we add the areas to find the total.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenE{800}+\purpleD{100} +\redD{18}=918$

So $2 \times 459 = 918$.

These are the areas:

$\begin{align}
&A=800\\\\
&B=100\\\\
&C=18\\\\
&2 \times 459=918
\end{align}$

options.alt: An area model using a rectangle of 3 unequal parts, all with a width of 2 but different lengths. From left to right, the parts are labeled A, B, and C, and the lengths, also from left to right, are 400, 50, and 9.
